title: Move Semantics 1
description: In Rust, when you assign a value to another variable or pass it to a function, ownership is moved. The original variable can no longer be used.
instructions: Fix the code so it compiles by understanding how ownership moves work.
hints: After `vec0` is passed to `fill_vec`, ownership is moved and `vec0` can no longer be used.
You can either clone vec0 before passing it, or remove the line that uses vec0 after the move.
Try: let vec1 = fill_vec(vec0.clone()); to keep vec0 usable.
explanation: When a value that doesn't implement the Copy trait (like Vec) is passed to a function, ownership is transferred (moved). The original variable becomes invalid. To keep both variables valid, you can clone the value with `.clone()`, which creates a deep copy. This is a fundamental concept in Rust's ownership system.
